steps.title: Use the given value of $x$
steps.content: The first equation already says $x=8$. Substitute that into the second equation: $$8+3y=26.$$

steps.title: Solve for $y$
steps.content: Subtract $8$ from both sides: $$3y=18.$$ Divide by $3$: $$y=6.$$

steps.title: Check in Desmos
steps.content: Graphing $x=8$ and $x+3y=26$ gives an intersection at $(8,6)$, so the requested $y$-value is 6.
